title: Hello World — Starting My Build in Public Journey
excerpt: Why I'm building in public and what I hope to achieve. Sharing the process, the failures, and the little wins along the way.

title: Little Wins — The iPhone App
excerpt: The backstory, tech stack, UI decisions, and why there's no login. Building the ADHD-friendly win tracker I've wanted for years.

title: Submitting Little Wins to the App Store — Current Status
excerpt: What's done, what's left, and why I keep procrastinating on screenshots and certificates. Shipping by end of week.

title: I Tried Android First — 12 Users, 15 Days, Blocked
excerpt: Android testing with real users was paralyzing for an introvert with ADHD. The iPhone App Store path doesn't need any of that. Here's why I pivoted.

title: Self-Diagnosed, Assuming ADHD — My Toolkit
excerpt: Pomodoro 15/3, XMind, Obsidian, hand-writing, stickies, brain dumps, and the interstitial journal. How I cope with frustration, scattered focus, and not finishing things.

title: My AI Coding Tool Journey — From Paid to Free
excerpt: ChatGPT → Windsurf (paid) → Antigravity (free/paid) → OpenCode + DeepSeek V4 Flash Free. Why I can't afford AI credits, what I use instead, and the Project Tracker app I built because I forget where I deploy things.

title: I Tried Flutter, Came Back to React Native + Expo
excerpt: Built one Android app in Flutter, never published it. Switched back to React Native + Expo for Little Wins. Dart vs JS, ecosystem size, dev experience, and why familiarity won.

title: Morning Ideas, Holding Hands
excerpt: Three ideas this morning, before breakfast. The ADHD morning flood, the capture ritual, and the ones that stick.

title: Daily Targets — July 24
excerpt: Steps done, zikr, and today's targets: 10 reels, idea validation, Little Wins App Store launch, YouTube update, and a gratitude app idea.

title: The Daily School Run — My Real Routine
excerpt: 8 trips a day: school runs, college drops, water cans. Building in public happens in the gaps. One commit between pickups.

title: The Weekly Vegetable Run — And Other Things I Drop
excerpt: Forgetting the market, missing Sunday meat, the exhaustion and depression behind it. Elder son helps from the local shop. Writing it down to remember next week.

title: The Care I Couldn't Give
excerpt: Someone dear is unwell. Tooth pain, low blood, tailbone. I haven't been able to give the time or money. Writing it down to stop avoiding it.

title: I Don't Know What Happened to My Brain
excerpt: Target: 9:30 AM App Store launch. Reality: 5 blog posts and zero screenshots. Task aversion, ADHD switching, or burnout — probably all three.

title: Xcode Build Failed: The fmt Pod Strikes Again
excerpt: New icon, UI improvements, clean TypeScript. Then the native build chain said no. 5 compiler errors in a C++ pod I never touched. The React Native tax.

title: Why CapCut — And Why Mac Mini for Reels
excerpt: How I chose CapCut for batch reel creation on Mac Mini. Requirements, why mobile doesn't scale to 10 reels, and today's target of 50 DMs.

title: Experimenting: Canva, VN Editor, and the CapCut That Wasn't There
excerpt: CapCut not found in India App Store. Tried Canva and VN Editor instead. ADHD tax paid in tool-switching. Settled on VN for today's 10 reels.

title: How I Build in Public — OpenCode + Desktop/Aichats Workflow
excerpt: Conversations with OpenCode → saved to ~/Desktop/aichats/ → turned into blog posts → committed → Vercel deploys. The ADHD-friendly publishing pipeline.

title: Nap at 1:45 — The ADHD Midday Reset
excerpt: Brain hits a wall at 1:45 PM. 15-20 minute nap, alarm set, then back to reels and coding. Non-negotiable part of the daily rhythm.

title: Gopi Called — PrepPilot, 2 Early Adopters, and a Missed Conversation
excerpt: Full WhatsApp conversation with Gopi — PrepPilot origin story: a father automating exam revision for his son. ICSE Classes 6-10, 2 early adopters. I couldn't engage properly because of my own App Store deadline.
